deep_analysis.executive_intro:
This FTSE screen is broadly cautious: 164 holds and 47 avoids dwarf 9 strong buys and 22 buys, so quantitatively attractive names are a small subset of the universe. All five top candidates are new strong buys (first week at signal) with conviction scores of 43–51%, passing all five model families (cheapness, quality, dividend, GARP, risk) on 19/20 metrics. Sector clustering is modest but visible: two industrials (MEGP.L, MGNS.L) sit alongside one each in communication services, consumer defensive, and healthcare. One caution: several models flag missing balance-sheet or cash-flow inputs (NCAV, operating cash flow), and every top name fails or skips Earnings Quality and Piotroski F-Score checks — headline value scores may overstate cash-flow durability.

**ITV.L (Communication Services)** — Flagged on 13/22 models (composite 87%, sector-relative 85%) with P/E 12.2, P/B 1.5, yield 6.8%, ROE 12.3%, and D/E 48%. Passes include Graham Enterprising, Earnings Yield, FCF Yield (9.1%), Lynch PEG (0.48), High Dividend Yield, Composite Value, and Financial Health; fails include Graham Defensive (current ratio 1.29), Buffett Quality (thin margins), Economic Moat, and Piotroski F-Score (2/9). Timing is the only “accumulate” signal in the group (RSI 34, below 200-day MA). No sector concentration issue among top picks. **Verdict: accumulate** — strongest composite and timing support, though quality/moat screens are mixed.

- **ITV.L:** Structural decline in linear TV and advertising cyclicality are not in the screen; regulatory/policy shifts (PSB obligations, Ofcom) and content-investment risk vs thin 6.3% margins are open questions. Piotroski F-Score 2/9 and failed Buffett Quality/Economic Moat suggest earnings quality and moat durability need manual review.
- **AEP.L:** Consumer defensive label may mask commodity or agricultural cyclicality (plantations); governance, land/asset revaluation policy, and pension/off-balance-sheet liabilities are not captured — insufficient data in JSON to assess.

- **MGNS.L:** UK construction and contracting cyclicality, project risk, and margin volatility (failed quality/moat on thin margins) are not fully captured; order-book concentration and pension deficits — insufficient data in JSON to confirm.

documents.name: AEP Plantations Plc
documents.executive_summary: AEP Plantations Plc is a London-listed Indonesian palm-oil producer that the quantitative screen rates as a **Strong Buy** (16/22 models; composite score 89%), supported by low multiples (P/E 9.1, P/B 1.5), a ~6.0% dividend yield, 15.1% ROE, and an exceptionally strong balance sheet (current ratio 8.7; negligible debt). With no downloadable primary filing bodies in this research pack, Yahoo-sourced financials (`financials_annual.json`) indicate accelerating FY2025 performance: revenue of $465.2m (+25% y/y) and net income of $90.9m (+35%), with free cash flow of $83.6m. The investment case rests on cash-generative estates, active capital return (dividends and buybacks), and optionality from a proposed Indonesian subsidiary IPO. The central debate is whether cheap valuation and shareholder-friendly capital allocation offset Indonesian export-policy risk, palm-oil price cyclicality, and limited primary-disclosure depth in this source pack.
documents.investment_thesis:
The screen’s Strong Buy signal aligns with classical value criteria. AEP passes all five model families—cheapness, quality, dividend, GARP, and risk—including Graham Defensive/Enterprising, FCF Yield, Buffett Quality, High Dividend Yield, Dividend Growth, Magic Formula, Acquirer’s Multiple, Dreman Contrarian, Composite Value, and Financial Health. At P/E 9.1 and P/B 1.46 (P/E×P/B ≈ 13.3, below Graham’s 22.5 threshold), the stock screens as materially undervalued relative to reported earnings power and book value.

Business quality indicators reinforce the signal: ROE of 15.1%, positive and growing FCF (screen: ~$76m; Yahoo FY2025: $83.6m), and a net-cash-style balance sheet with total debt of $0.5m against equity of $580.7m and cash of $231.8m (Yahoo FY2025). The current ratio of 8.69 implies ample liquidity to fund dividends, the £8m buyback programme announced in July 2026, and estate capex without balance-sheet stress. Conviction on the screen signal is still early (one week at Strong Buy; conviction score 50%); timing is neutral (RSI ~51, price roughly in line with the 200-day moving average).

For a value investor, the hook is a cash-rich, low-leverage plantation asset trading at a single-digit earnings multiple with a meaningful yield, whilst management actively returns capital (FY2025 dividends paid: $34.6m; share repurchases of $11.4m per Yahoo, with ongoing buybacks per news flow). Recent M&A (Pinago Utama, ~$162m, May 2025) and a proposed IPO of subsidiary PT AEP Nusantara Plantations (April 2026) add strategic optionality beyond the core dividend-and-buyback story. Sector-relative composite score (79%) exceeds the broader Consumer Defensive peer set, suggesting the discount is partly idiosyncratic rather than purely sector-driven.
documents.financial_review:
**Primary source status:** `gap_fill_source_map.json` confirms `filings_bodies: false` (0 body files). The filings index lists 31 RNS items (`annual: 0`, `interim: 2`, `with_body: 0`), including “FY25 Results” (30 April 2026, classified `other`) and interim headlines only. **All figures below fall back to Yahoo `financials_annual.json`**, cross-checked against `screening_snapshot.json` where noted. `macro_context.json` is absent.

**Income and cyclicality (Yahoo):** Revenue rebounded in FY2025 to $465.2m (+25% y/y) after a two-year trough ($370–372m in FY2023–FY2024), following the FY2022 peak of $447.6m. Net income rose to $90.9m (+35% y/y); operating income $111.9m. The pattern confirms plantation/commodity earnings sensitivity rather than stable “defensive” cash flows — consistent with May 2026 Indonesia export headlines in `news_manifest.json` / `alternate_news.json`.

**Cash generation (Yahoo):** FY2025 OCF $113.8m, capex $30.2m, **FCF $83.6m** (screen FCF ~$76m — directionally aligned). Dividends paid $34.6m (~38% payout); buybacks $11.4m. Interest income $8.0m vs expense $44k corroborates net-cash economics.

**Balance sheet — gap-fill resolution on D/E and current ratio (Yahoo FY2025):**

| Item | FY2025 (USD) | Implication |
|------|--------------|-------------|
| Total debt | $0.5m | Capital leases only → D/E ≈0% |
| Cash + ST investments | $254.3m | Net-cash position |
| Taxes receivable | $46.9m | Inflates current assets / CR |
| Current assets / liabilities | $344.3m / $39.6m | CR ≈8.7 (matches screen) |
| Pension (non-current) | $8.0m | Modest; on balance sheet |
| Net PPE (land $54.5m gross basis) | $158.6m | No revaluation policy disclosed |

The zero D/E and high current ratio reflect **genuine balance-sheet conservatism**, not a data artefact. Composition risk: liquidity is concentrated in cash and tax receivables rather than low working-capital turnover.

**M&A / structure (news + Yahoo gap):** Alternate news records Pinago Utama completion at IDR 2.8 trillion (~$162m headline, marketscreener.com, 5 May 2025) and a proposed IPO of PT AEP Nusantara Plantations (filings index, 16 April 2026). Yahoo FY2025 shows only $0.3m goodwill/intangibles and negligible acquisition cash outflow — **post-deal accounts are not captured** in the Yahoo snapshot.

**Interim / filings (headlines only):** AGM Trading Update (15 June 2026) paired with “mixed production in first five months” (Investing.com UK, 15 June 2026); Trading Statement (22 October 2024) — no body text. Alternate news themed pulls for “annual report” / “interim results” returned no statutory PDF content.

**Remaining gaps:** No primary going-concern, covenant, contingent liability, segment, land-valuation, or governance disclosure. Pinago integration economics unverified. Share-count discontinuity (39.3m vs ~395m diluted shares YoY) tied to the June 2026 10-for-1 split (news) requires adjusted comparables.

---
documents.risks_and_flags:
**Evidenced (local sources):**

- **Commodity / sector misclassification:** Revenue swing and Indonesia export sensitivity (Yahoo + “Palm oil stocks struggle after Indonesian intervention”, Investors’ Chronicle, 27 May 2026; “AEP shares fall as Indonesia announces export controls”, London South East, 20 May 2026). Consumer Defensive peer ranking overstates stability.
- **Regulatory:** RNS “Indonesian Government Export Proposals” (20 May 2026) — impact unquantified without body text.
- **Operational:** “Mixed production in first five months” (Investing.com UK, 15 June 2026) — uneven H1 2026 output.
- **M&A execution:** Pinago deal ($162m / IDR 2.8tn headlines, May 2025) vs minimal Yahoo FY2025 acquisition accounting — integration/funding risk open.
- **Capital structure actions:** 10-for-1 split (June 2026) and £8m buyback (July 2026, news) tighten free float; volatility risk in a small cap.
- **Screen limitations:** Passes 16/22 but fails Lynch PEG, Piotroski (6/9), and Earnings Quality per red-flag pass; Yahoo shows OCF, suggesting possible screen data gap on earnings quality.
- **Data noise:** NASDAQ:AEP utility stories pollute news/filings index; several RNS items appear unrelated to AEP Plantations.

**Partially evidenced:**

- **Pension:** $8.0m non-current obligation on Yahoo FY2025 — modest but not fully actuarial without statutory notes.
- **Balance-sheet strength:** Net-cash profile confirmed (see Q2); not a red flag, but taxes-receivable concentration warrants monitoring.

**Still open (requires alternate sources):**

- **Governance / related parties / Indonesia structure:** “Change of Name” RNS (25 November 2025) and proposed subsidiary IPO imply complexity; no board, control, or related-party detail locally. *Close with:* Companies House accounts + annual report governance section.
- **Land / asset revaluation and hidden liabilities:** PPE at depreciated cost in Yahoo; no fair-value policy, lease commitments beyond capitalised leases, or contingencies. *Close with:* Companies House PDF notes + IR presentation estate tables.
- **Going concern / covenants:** No filing body language available. *Close with:* Investegate RNS full-text re-pull of “FY25 Results” and statutory accounts.

---
documents.news_highlights:
Coverage over the past year is moderate but focused on capital allocation, M&A, and Indonesia policy. Material items (excluding misattributed US utility “AEP” stories):

- **Share buyback programme:** “AEP Plantations Launches £8 Million Share Buyback Programme” (TipRanks, 5 July 2026); ongoing repurchases through mid-July 2026 (TipRanks/Kalkine Media, 10–17 July 2026); RNS “Share Buyback Programme” (6 July 2026, headline only).
- **Share split:** “AEP Plantations to Enact 10-for-1 Share Split to Boost Liquidity” (TipRanks, 21 June 2026); “IN BRIEF: AEP Plantations to implement 10-for-1 share split” (London South East, 22 June 2026).
- **AGM / trading update:** “AEP Plantations reports mixed production in first five months” (Investing.com UK, 15 June 2026); RNS “AGM Trading Update” and “Result of AGM” (15 June 2026).
- **Indonesia export policy:** RNS “Indonesian Government Export Proposals” (20 May 2026); “AEP shares fall as Indonesia announces export controls for palm oil” (London South East, 20 May 2026); counter-narrative “Indonesian export shake-up hands investors a buying opportunity in London-listed palm oil producers” (Yahoo Finance UK, 21 May 2026).
- **M&A:** “AEP Plantations buys Indonesian agribusiness Pinago for USD162 million” (London South East, 5 May 2025); “AEP Plantations Expands Indonesian Footprint With Pinago Utama Acquisition” (Yahoo Finance UK, 5 May 2025); “CB Industrial exits Indonesian plantation venture with RM38m divestment to UK-listed AEP” (The Edge Malaysia, 14 October 2025).
- **Indonesia IPO optionality:** RNS “Proposed IPO of PT AEP Nusantara Plantations Tbk” (16 April 2026); “UK-based AEP Plantations subsidiary eyes Indonesia IPO” (IDNFinancials.com, 18 April 2026).
- **FY2025 results:** RNS “FY25 Results” (30 April 2026, no body).
- **Corporate:** RNS “Change of Name” (25 November 2025).
- **Third-party interest:** “Why I’m buying this cash-generation machine” (Investors’ Chronicle, 13 January 2026); “Estimating The Intrinsic Value Of AEP Plantations Plc” (Yahoo Finance, 19 December 2025).

News coverage is adequate for corporate actions but thin on detailed operational and regulatory analysis. Several indexed RNS items (PDMR notifications, holdings disclosures, 20 July 2026) are routine and add little strategic insight.
documents.research_rationale: Gap-fill confirms the screen’s net-cash balance sheet and value metrics are economically grounded, but primary disclosure remains too thin to fully validate governance, cyclicality overlays, or post-Pinago accounts, leaving the Strong Buy case supported yet not fully de-risked.

documents.name: Hikma Pharmaceuticals PLC
documents.executive_summary:
Hikma Pharmaceuticals is a London-listed, US-dollar-reporting generics and injectables group trading on a value screen of P/E 11.2, P/B 1.8, dividend yield 4.0%, and ROE 16.5% — metrics that place it in the top decile of UK healthcare on the quantitative model. The investment case rests on a diversified three-division franchise (Injectables, Generics, Branded), earnings recovery from 2023 impairment trough, and capital return via dividends and a buyback launched in February 2026. The central debate is whether February 2026’s guidance reset and working-capital-heavy cash flow are transient execution noise or evidence of structural margin and US pricing pressure. Primary filing bodies were not available in the research corpus, limiting verification of management guidance, contingencies, and covenant language.

---
documents.investment_thesis:
The quantitative screen flags Hikma as a **Strong Buy**, passing 13 of 22 models across five families: cheapness, quality, dividend, GARP, and risk. Standout passes include Graham Enterprising, Lynch PEG, Neff PEGY, Buffett Quality, Magic Formula, Acquirer’s Multiple, and Financial Health — a profile consistent with a profitable, cash-generative compounder trading below intrinsic multiples rather than a distressed cyclical.

**Valuation.** At P/E 11.2 and earnings yield ~9%, the shares embed little growth despite revenue compounding at roughly 7% annually over 2022–2025 (Yahoo fallback). P/B 1.8 on ROE 16.5% suggests the market prices Hikma closer to a mature generic manufacturer than a quality compounder with injectables exposure.

**Business quality.** Hikma operates across injectables (hospital channel, higher barriers), US/EU/MENA generics, and a smaller branded portfolio — a structure that diversifies patent-cliff and pricing risk relative to single-product peers. Normalised earnings have recovered sharply: reported net income rose from $190m (2023) to $359m (2024) and $402m (2025), with diluted EPS of $1.81 (2025) versus $0.85 (2023).

**Shareholder returns.** Dividends paid rose from $137m (2023) to $185m (2025). News flow confirms an active buyback programme from February 2026 and ongoing treasury purchases through July 2026. Chairman Victoria Hull increased her holding materially in May 2025 — a modest governance positive, though not dispositive.

**Why value investors should care.** The screen combines cheap absolute multiples with quality and dividend support — rare in healthcare. If 2026 guidance (reiterated in April 2026 per news) holds and free cash flow normalises after 2025 working-capital absorption, the risk/reward skews favourable without requiring heroic growth assumptions.

---
documents.financial_review:
**Source status.** `filings/bodies/` empty. `filings_index.json`: 24 items, all `other`, `with_body: 0`. Themed alternate news (pension/covenant/WC queries) returned no usable disclosure text. **Figures from Yahoo `financials_annual.json` and news headlines; USD unless noted.**

**Earnings vs FCF.** Screen trailing FCF **−$66.1m** (FCF yield ≈ −2.0%) sits alongside FY2025 net income **$402m** (+12% YoY) and FCF **$119m** (down from $329m). The bridge is working capital **−$251m**, CapEx **$317m**, and dividends **$185m** exceeding FCF. Trailing negativity implies H1 2026 quarters are more cash-consumptive than the FY average — unverified without interim filing bodies or quarterly cash data.

**Screen failures (evidenced).** FCF Yield (negative trailing FCF); Economic Moat (ROE 16.5% < 18%, D/E 62% > 50%); Piotroski 4/9 (leverage up, gross margin and asset turnover down YoY on Yahoo); High Dividend Yield (4.0% below absolute hurdle despite composite dividend-family pass).

**Dividend.** OCF covers dividends ~2.4×; FCF does not. Buyback (Feb 2026 launch per filings index) adds a second cash call. **Dividend safety through the next generic cycle: open.**

**Guidance (news).** Feb 2026 cut (*Investors' Chronicle*, 26 Feb); Apr 2026 reiteration after 503B exit (*DirectorsTalk* / *Investing.com*, 23 Apr; Yahoo Finance, 24 Apr). Magnitude unverified.

**Gaps:** segment FCF, covenants, pensions, going-concern, pipeline cliff schedule.

---
documents.risks_and_flags:
**Evidenced:** trailing FCF negative; FY FCF below dividends; net debt $1,334m (+$273m YoY); Xyrem settlement (filings index); tafamidis delay to 2031 (Pfizer settlement news); Feb/Apr 2026 guidance volatility; recurring impairments (Yahoo); gross margin compression 2024→2025.

**Open (source to close):** FDA enforcement specifics → RNS/FDA database; debt covenants → Companies House accounts; pension → statutory accounts note; product-level patent cliffs → IR pipeline deck; WC normalisation plan → IR presentation cash-flow bridge; dividend policy through cycle → annual report policy section.

**Screen blind spot:** Strong Buy composite does not cap the signal when trailing FCF is negative while dividends and buybacks run concurrently.

---
documents.news_highlights:
Coverage over the past year is **moderate in quantity but uneven in quality** — many SEO-driven Kalkine/Ad-hoc-news pieces alongside substantive sell-side and corporate items.

**Strategy and operations**
- *Hikma Pharmaceuticals Reiterates 2026 Guidance, Exits 503B Compounding in April Trading Update Call* (Yahoo Finance, 24 Apr 2026) — operational portfolio pruning; guidance maintained.
- *Hikma Pharmaceuticals PLC outlines growth strategy as global generics demand expands* (Ad-hoc-news.de, 9 Jul 2026).
- UK footprint expansion / NHS supply office push (Kalkine Media, 16–19 Jul 2026).

**Guidance shock and recovery**
- *Hikma’s shares plummet after guidance cut* (Investors’ Chronicle, 26 Feb 2026).
- *Hikma Pharma tumbles on softer guidance as buyback fails to soften pill* (Yahoo Finance UK, 26 Feb 2026).
- *Launch of Buyback Programme* (Investegate, 26 Feb 2026) — capital return response.

**Sell-side and sentiment**
- *Citi starts Hikma at 'buy' with strategy reset offering compelling entry point* (Yahoo Finance / Proactive Investors, 16 Jul 2026) — new coverage, constructive SOTP view.
- Multiple Yahoo Finance pieces (Mar–May 2026) document analyst target resets to £16–£20 cluster after the guidance event; JPMorgan cited at Overweight / £20.

**Legal / regulatory**
- *Settlement agreement for Xyrem® US lawsuits* (Investegate, 8 May 2025).
- *Pfizer (PFE) Reaches Settlement to Extend VYNDAMAX Patent Protection Until 2031* (Yahoo Finance, 28–30 Apr 2026) — Hikma named as settlement party.
- *Why Is Hikma Pharmaceuticals Suddenly In The Regulatory Crosshairs?* (Kalkine Media, 14 Jul 2026) — unverified detail.

**Corporate / governance**
- *Independent Chairman Victoria Hull Buys 241% More Shares* (simplywall.st, 2 May 2025).
- *Hikma Pharmaceuticals PLC(LSE:HIK) added to FTSE 250* (marketscreener.com, 23 Mar 2026) — index inclusion (Ex Investment Companies).
- Ongoing *Transaction in Own Shares* notices (Jul 2026) under active buyback.

**Sector context**
- *Generic drugmakers gain key victory in ‘skinny label’ patent case* (Yahoo Finance, 5 Jun 2026) — favourable industry precedent.

documents.name: ME Group International plc
documents.executive_summary:
ME Group International operates a global network of photobooths and unattended laundry machines, generating recurring, asset-heavy cash flows from high-footfall locations. The quantitative screen flags a **strong buy** on cheapness (P/E 7.6, yield 7.3%), quality (ROE 28.2%), and balance-sheet strength (debt/equity ~28%), yet the shares sit below their 200-day moving average after a sharp June 2026 sell-off tied to a profit-guidance cut. The central debate is whether trailing financial strength and laundry expansion offset cyclical photobooth softness, governance scrutiny, and a dividend that now exceeds reported free cash flow. Primary RNS filing bodies were not available in the source pack, limiting verification of management’s revised FY2026 outlook.

---
documents.investment_thesis:
The screen’s **strong buy** (14/22 models passed; composite score 78%; sector-relative 87%) rests on a rare combination for a UK small/mid-cap industrial: deep value multiples, high returns on equity, a covered-looking earnings yield, and passing scores across cheapness, quality, dividend, GARP, and risk families. Yahoo-sourced financials (fallback — see Financial Review) show five years of revenue and profit growth, material deleveraging, and operating cash conversion well above net income.

For a value investor, the hook is a profitable, installed-base business trading at roughly **7–8× earnings** with a **7%+ dividend yield**, where capital is redeployed into laundry roll-out and booth refresh rather than speculative growth. The laundry segment — including the April 2026 Asda collaboration — offers a second leg that is less discretionary than photobooth usage and is currently offsetting weaker booth trading per recent news flow. Peel Hunt reiterated a buy rating on 13 July 2026, aligning with the quantitative signal despite near-term guidance uncertainty.

The screen’s **new** signal (one week at strong buy; conviction 49%) and **neutral timing** (RSI ~57, price below 200-day MA) suggest fundamental appeal without a confirmed price inflection — appropriate for phased accumulation rather than aggressive chasing.

---
documents.financial_review:
**Primary source gap unchanged:** `filings/bodies/` contains **zero** extracts; `filings_index.json` lists 52 RNS items (`with_body: 0`) including interim *Trading Update* (1 Jun 2026) and *Q1 2026 Financial Statements — Unaudited* (13 Jul 2026), but no downloadable text. Several indexed headlines appear mis-attributed (e.g. Abri Group, SEGRO). **All figures below cite Yahoo `financials_annual.json` or screen fetch unless noted.**

### Trailing statutory trend (Yahoo fallback; FY September)

| Metric | FY2023 | FY2024 | FY2025 |
|--------|--------|--------|--------|
| Revenue (£m) | 297.7 | 307.9 | 315.4 |
| Net income (£m) | 50.7 | 54.1 | 56.6 |
| Diluted EPS (p) | 13.31 | 14.27 | 14.91 |
| Operating cash flow (£m) | 87.7 | 85.9 | 90.8 |
| Free cash flow (£m) | 33.7 | 31.3 | 25.2 |
| Cash dividends (£m) | 23.4 | 27.8 | 29.8 |
| Total debt (£m) | 90.5 | 59.8 | 43.0 |
| Cash (£m) | 103.7 | 77.5 | 56.5 |

Statutory earnings **continued to grow** through FY2025 (+4.6% net income YoY), but **FCF fell** for a third year (£33.7m → £25.2m) as capex rose to **£65.6m**. FY2025 **dividends exceeded FCF** (cover 0.84×) while **OCF cover remained ~3.0×** — the dividend sustainability debate hinges on whether investors trust operating cash (maintenance capex timing) or reported FCF (after growth capex).

### Screen-time metrics (fetch / `screening_snapshot.json`)

- **P/E 7.6**, **P/B 2.2**, **yield 7.3%**, **ROE 28.2%**, **D/E 28%**
- **Earnings growth −3.9%** (yfinance trailing; contradicts statutory FY2025 growth — likely reflects H1/guidance momentum)
- **FCF yield 3.6%** (TTM FCF ~£15.6m vs market cap ~£429m) — **fails** FCF Yield model (5% threshold)
- **Piotroski 4/9** — fails ROA improvement, OCF>NI, current-ratio improvement, asset-turnover improvement; partly distorted because **operating cash flow is not populated** in the fetch row despite Yahoo showing £90.8m

### Interim colour (news / alternate news only — not verified from RNS bodies)

- **1 Jun 2026:** H1 FY2026 revenue reportedly **+2%**; profit guidance **lowered** after weak April consumer demand (France); shares fell ~22% (*ME Group shares fall 22%…*, Yahoo Finance UK).
- **13 Jul 2026:** H1 results narrative — **laundry drives EBITDA growth**; **photobooth softness** and **lower equipment sales**; management **on track for revised FY profit** (*ME Group International H1 Earnings Call Highlights*, yfinance; *MEGP: Laundry growth offset photobooth softness…*, TradingView).
- **13 Jul 2026:** Alternate news flags **profit before tax and cash generation declined** in H1 (*MEGP: Laundry-driven growth lifted EBITDA, but profit before tax and cash generation declined*, TradingView) — supports screen momentum flags but **no primary figures** available locally.

### Remaining gaps

- No audited/interim RNS body for H1 PBT, EPS, segment revenue, or dividend declaration
- No covenant, pension, or going-concern language from filings
- `macro_context.json` absent
- `quarterly_income` empty in Yahoo cache

documents.research_rationale: Gap-fill confirms the quant cheapness/quality case but strengthens red flags on negative earnings momentum, sub-5% FCF yield, H1 cash-generation pressure, and absent primary filings, weakening the strong-buy signal without fully invalidating trailing balance-sheet strength.

weekly_updates.summary:
Q: Photo-booth/industrial services revenue is highly cyclical and venue-traffic dependent; negative earnings growth (-3.9%) and weak Piotroski (4/9) may signal a peak-earnings trap; dividend sustainability at 7.3% yield with 3.6% FCF yield is an open question.
Status: partially_resolved
Evidence: Live fetch via `screening_snapshot.json` / fetch pipeline confirms **earnings growth −3.9%**, **FCF yield 3.6%** (FCF ~£15.6m vs market cap ~£429m), and **Piotroski 4/9** (fails on ROA, OCF>NI, current ratio, asset turnover; passes on positive NI, leverage decline, no dilution, gross margin). Yahoo `financials_annual.json` shows statutory net income still rose **+4.6%** FY2024→FY2025, so the −3.9% figure reflects yfinance trailing/forward momentum, not full-year accounts — a peak-earnings read is plausible but not proven. Alternate news (*MEGP: Laundry-driven growth lifted EBITDA, but profit before tax and cash generation declined*, TradingView, 13 Jul 2026) corroborates near-term cash/earnings pressure despite laundry EBITDA growth.
SourcesTried: filings_bodies, filings_index, yahoo_financials, news_manifest, alternate_news, screening_snapshot
NextSources: Company IR / results presentation PDF (`gap_fill_source_map.json` → `company_ir_presentation`) for H1 PBT, segment cash flow, and capex/dividend policy; Companies House annual report PDF for dividend cover and going-concern language

Q: Is the 7.3% yield sustainable given -3.9% earnings growth, 3.6% FCF yield, and a price downtrend below the 200-day MA?
Status: partially_resolved
Evidence: `screening_snapshot.json` shows **yield 7.3%**, **P/E 7.6**, **price ~16% below 200-day MA**, neutral timing. Yahoo fallback: FY2025 **dividends £29.8m** vs **FCF £25.2m** (cover 0.84×) but **operating cash flow £90.8m** (cover ~3.0×); screen TTM FCF ~£15.6m implies cover closer to **0.5×** at current payout. June 2026 trading-update headlines (*ME Group Lowers 2026 Profit Expectations…*, Yahoo Finance UK, 1 Jun 2026) and July H1 cash-generation decline (alternate news) argue payout headroom is tightening, but no RNS body states a cut or policy change.
SourcesTried: filings_bodies, filings_index, yahoo_financials, news_manifest, alternate_news, screening_snapshot
NextSources: Company IR presentation PDF for interim dividend declaration and management payout guidance; full Investegate RNS re-pull for H1 2026 cash-flow statement

Q: (Industrials) — 14/22 models (composite 78%, sector-relative 87%) with P/E 7.6, P/B 2.2, yield 7.3%, ROE 28.2%, D/E 28%. Passes Low P/E + High Yield, Buffett Quality, Economic Moat, High Dividend Yield, Magic Formula, and Financial Health; fails FCF Yield (3.6%), Lynch PEG (negative earnings growth -3.9%), and Piotroski F-Score (4/9). Price is below 200-day MA in a downtrend despite neutral timing. Sector concentration risk: one of two industrials in the top five. Verdict: watchlist — valuation and quality metrics are compelling, but shrinking earnings and sub-5% FCF yield reduce confidence.
Status: partially_resolved
Evidence: `screening_snapshot.json` and live model evaluation confirm pass/fail split: **14/22 passed** including High Dividend Yield and Financial Health; **FCF Yield**, **Lynch PEG**, and **Piotroski F-Score** fail at stated thresholds. Conviction **49%**, signal **new** (1 week). Price **16% below 200-day MA**, RSI ~57, timing **neutral**. MEGP ranks **4th of top-five** strong buys in `output/deep_analysis_payload.json`; **MGNS.L** is the second industrial (5th). Qualitative overlay aligns with **watchlist/accumulate with caution**, not full conviction.
SourcesTried: filings_bodies, filings_index, yahoo_financials, news_manifest, alternate_news, screening_snapshot
NextSources: Company IR presentation PDF to reconcile screen momentum (−3.9%) with statutory FY2025 growth (+4.6%); exchange filing full-text re-pull for interim segment margins

Q: MGNS.L (Industrials) — Fewest passes at 11/22 (composite 71%, sector-relative 75%) with P/E 13.3, P/B 3.0, yield 3.3%, ROE 25.1%, FCF yield 6.1%, D/E 18%. Passes Graham Enterprising, FCF Yield, Lynch PEG (0.49), Quality Value, Buffett Quality, Magic Formula, and Financial Health; fails Earnings Yield (7.5%), Low P/E + High Yield, Economic Moat (thin margins), High Dividend Yield, and Dreman Contrarian. Lowest conviction in the group (43%). Sector concentration risk: second industrial name alongside MEGP.L. Verdict: watchlist — solid ROE and leverage profile, but weaker pass rate and margin flags vs peers.
Status: partially_resolved
Evidence: `output/deep_analysis_payload.json` top-candidate record for **MGNS.L** matches quoted metrics (**11/22**, composite **71%**, conviction **43%**, passes include **FCF Yield** and **Lynch PEG**). MEGP.L scores higher on cheapness/yield (**14/22**, **78%**, **49%** conviction) but fails FCF Yield and Lynch PEG where MGNS passes — the two industrials are **complementary, not redundant**: MEGP is cheaper income with weaker cash-flow momentum; MGNS is higher-quality cash conversion at a richer multiple. Sector clustering (2/5 top names) remains a portfolio-level consideration, not resolved by company-specific sources.
SourcesTried: screening_snapshot (MEGP.L only local); cross-reference via deep_analysis_payload.json for MGNS.L
NextSources: MGNS.L company IR / half-year RNS (separate research pack) if comparing industrial pair trade; none required to close MEGP-specific gap

Q: This FTSE screen is broadly cautious: 164 holds and 47 avoids dwarf 9 strong buys and 22 buys, so quantitatively attractive names are a small subset of the universe. All five top candidates are new strong buys (first week at signal) with conviction scores of 43–51%, passing all five model families (cheapness, quality, dividend, GARP, risk) on 19/20 metrics. Sector clustering is modest but visible: two industrials (MEGP.L, MGNS.L) sit alongside one each in communication services, consumer defensive, and healthcare. One caution: several models flag missing balance-sheet or cash-flow inputs (NCAV, operating cash flow), and every top name fails or skips Earnings Quality and Piotroski F-Score checks — headline value scores may overstate cash-flow durability.
Status: partially_resolved
Evidence: `output/deep_analysis_payload.json` confirms **signal_distribution**: 9 strong buy, 22 buy, 164 hold, 47 avoid; top five all **strong_buy**, **new**, conviction **43–51%**, all pass **five factor families**. For MEGP specifically, live fetch shows **`operating_cashflow` is None** in the metrics row despite Yahoo FY2025 OCF £90.8m — explaining Piotroski/Earnings Quality failures driven by **missing inputs**, not necessarily weak cash generation. FCF Yield fail (3.6%) is substantive, not a data gap.
SourcesTried: screening_snapshot, macro_context (absent), fetch pipeline validation
NextSources: Ingest fix for Yahoo operating cash flow into `CompanyMetrics` (pipeline change, not external source); Companies House accounts for NCAV verification on MEGP.L
